parsed_review.summary: CP00-396 is a clean, descriptor-only continuation of the RP13 payments-core series (CP00-392..395). It covers exactly 150 RP13 units (RP13.P02.M06.S13 -> RP13.P03.M05.S04), risk class C, deviation_from_plan=false, with deliverable/micro-title distributions that agree across the live plan, manifest plan_binding_snapshot, review-baseline, registry requirements, and manifest delivery_counts, and are enforced by validatePaymentsCoreCp396Coverage. The implementation (registry.js/service.js/validators.js) produces only frozen descriptor objects via pure functions with exhaustive no-write/no-runtime/no-real-data flags; there are no state writes, no DB/object-storage access, no Hermes runtime receipts, no payments/matching/AR-aging/journal runtime, and no command/UI/API execution. Tests (model.test.js:313-371) and the contract validator (validate-rp13-payments-core-contract.mjs:272-312, deep-equality on contract projections) cover coverage, descriptor-only rows, authority boundaries, evidence packets, and the CP00-397 handoff. The production_ready claim is appropriately conservative: manifest and command-evidence both set production_ready=false with status implemented_pending_review, exposing only production_ready_candidate=true at the Hermes packet level. The authority boundary holds: claude_is_final_approval=false, enterprise_trust_not_claimed_from_local_validation=true, human_final_approval_required=true; the review request is read-only (Read/Grep/Glob) and Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic review packet/receipt descriptors, not product runtime. Review-evidence hardening checks pass: auth preflight succeeded with valid JSON (no auth failure, no 0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped output), and no invalid review attempts are recorded. Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are absent at review time by design, matching the closed CP00-395 structure and the command-evidence post-review-gated-command notes; this is expected, not missing evidence. The 7 modified tracked files match the active implementation refs (index.js needs no change due to export *), active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty, and unrelated dirty files are correctly excluded. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; one cosmetic P3 on scope-descriptor labeling. Recommend proceeding to post-review adjudication and human final approval (Claude does not grant final approval or enterprise-trust closure).

findings.title: changed-file-scope lists the pack's own output dir as both implementation and unrelated-preserved
findings.details: docs/closeout-packs/cp00-396/ (the pack's own manifest/command-evidence output directory) appears simultaneously in untracked_implementation_files (line 132) and in unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (line 138). The same dual-listing applies to docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md. This is a cosmetic inconsistency in the scope categorization: a directory cannot be both an in-scope implementation artifact and an 'unrelated' preserved file. It has no effect on review integrity because the decision-relevant field active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty (nothing in-scope was hidden from review), and the substantive pack outputs were inspected directly.
findings.recommendation: In the changed-file-scope generator, treat the pack's own docs/closeout-packs/<pack>/ directory and pack handoff docs as in-scope implementation/output artifacts only, and exclude them from unrelated_dirty_files_preserved so the two lists are mutually exclusive. Non-blocking for CP00-396 closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Open, non-blocking. Cosmetic metadata-only labeling issue in the review scope descriptor; does not affect pack production-readiness, no-write/no-real-data boundaries, or closeout gating.
